{{Quote|That was the whole philosophic basis for the Emperor's New Order in the first place. Alone of all those in the Imperial Senate, he understood that so many diverse species and cultures could never live together without a strong hand governing them.|Moff Andray, speaking to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Pellaeon and his fellow Moffs|<ref name="Specter of the Past" />}}
'''Andray''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sentience/Legends|individual]] who served as [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of one of the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s remaining eight [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] near the end of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], he took part in a meeting in Moff [[Vilim Disra|Disra's]] [[Disra's palace|palace]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]], in which [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] described his plan to open peace talks with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to the remaining Moffs. After the meeting, Andray reluctantly supported opening peace talks.

==Biography==
{{Quote|You must be joking, Admiral Pellaeon.|Andray, after hearing Pellaeon's proposal|<ref name="Specter of the Past" />}}
Andray was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] who [[Governor/Legends|governed]] one of the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s eight remaining [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and who served on the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], the governing body of the [[Imperial Remnant]], near the end of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Specter of the Past" /> In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].</ref> he, along with the other remaining Moffs, took part in a meeting in the conference room of Moff [[Vilim Disra|Disra's]] [[Disra's palace|palace]] on the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], in which [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] described his plan to open peace talks with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Once Pellaeon had laid out his plan, Andray initially believed that he was joking but continued to listen to him alongside the other Moffs. Moff [[Bemos]] suggested that, given [[Time/Legends|time]], the New Republic would collapse on its own, and Andray agreed. Although Pellaeon agreed that the New Republic would eventually collapse, he also believed that it would ensure that the remnants of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] were eliminated first.<ref name="Specter of the Past" />

After more than an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]], the meeting concluded, and Andray, along with the other Moffs, reluctantly agreed to support peace talks.<ref name="Specter of the Past" /> The Moffs also provided Pellaeon with their credentials, which he later presented to [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] of the New Republic. Pellaeon's push for peace talks resulted in the signing of the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty]] peace accords, which he signed with New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Ponc Gavrisom]] and which signified the end of the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="Votf">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Andray was fiercely loyal to the ideals of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]].<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|30-31|Andray, Moff}}</ref> He believed in the philosophic basis behind the New Order, namely that the diverse [[Species/Legends|species]] and cultures present within the Empire could not live together without a strong hand to govern them.<ref name="Specter of the Past" />

==Behind the scenes==
Andray first appeared in the [[1997]] novel ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', which was written by [[Timothy Zahn]].<ref name="Specter of the Past" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}}
